Along the way, you come across what used to be a meeting room fitted with broken tables and papers spread all around the dust-filled place. Large containers full of water and local plants take up a large corner of the room near neatly stored rations and biscuits. There’s no doubt somebody lives here, somebody strong enough to push sydians, raskvels, and sex-crazed VIs away. You continue on through the obscured tunnels until you reach the abandoned ship’s helm. A few consoles flicker to life when they detect your presence, allowing you to search for leftover data in the ship’s mainframe. A quick survey through the manifest indicates you are aboard what used to be The Gloaming, a pirate transport ship traveling between freshly discovered rush worlds with smuggled goods. Data indicates it was shot down by the Black Void in Tarkus’s orbit months ago, killing most of the crew — except for a couple of weird creatures labeled as Haywire Alien Cyborg and Toxic Jellyfish. Despite all your efforts, you can’t find any further data on these aliens before the power runs out, stopping your electronic adventures. Dim noises come from below, where the ship’s reactor ought to be. With your [pc.weapon] in hand, you follow a makeshift dolomite ledge replacing the melted stairs towards the ominously glowing room housing what you expect to be a broken core. To your surprise, the plasma engine’s still active and fueled with a cell connected to an array of solar panels and an improvised water purifier. Weak lights draw your attention towards a big nest of cushions and blankets. The imposing [silly |pillow fort |lair ] is inhabited by a snoring blue alien with three closed eyes. A loud gasp crosses your lips when you realize that the ship’s inhabitant is actually a [var0|Leyak|disembodied head with lash-like tentacles] resting peacefully in the plush paradise. Your muffled sound of surprise manages to wake the creature. The teal floating head’s trio of eyes look at you with a mix of terror and surprise whilst a red targeting laser climbs your [pc.legs] up to your [pc.chest] before setting on your forehead. “Galactic citizen [pc.name] Steele, drop your weapon and move away from my friend! You have fifteen seconds to comply,” a loud, feminine voice altered by bionic amplifiers orders. Your [pc.eyes] spot a powerful, cybernetic frame of an imposing creature. The slowly charging Pyrexxx Annihilator serving as her right arm is locked on your face, ready to turn you into a charred pile of ashes. The bionic alien glances at her terrified friend then at you again. Maybe peacefully following her orders will save you some trouble, but in the end, it’s up to you to decide if you want to live or if you want to fight. ]

It would be nice if she could provide you some context. ] The purple girl’s features sadden for a handful of seconds before going back to a more relaxed facade. “Well, it’s not a very happy tale, but it’s worth listening to I guess,” she mutters, [silly |snuggling herself into a large, blanket fort |taking a more comfy position on her soft cushions ]. “My name is Paulette and I’m a zaika[var0 |. I guess I’m not the first of my kin you met, |, ]” she says, placing a lithe, chromed hand on her chest. “I was born among the populace of our tidally-locked homeworld, Dhaal. I was raised from hatchling to a full grown [silly |space drow |lady ] with a dozen other sisters by Kemmie, a very gentle and kind [silly |rental mommy |adoptive mother ].” She stops her story a moment to clear her misty eyes and takes a couple of deep breaths before speaking again. “[silly |Architect |Stars ] above, I miss her so much. A few weeks after I reached maturity, I had to leave her home to move to the roaring factories, else she would have lost her mysterious benefactor’s funds.” Wait, can’t she try to find a way to go back there now? The violet lady sadly nods before setting her gaze back on you. “She died a couple of years later and I had to quit my job to be able to go to her funeral. My sisters were here too and followed in her footsteps, helping the poorest of us the best they could. I sold my body to a Peer a couple of days later out of desperation; it was just too hard to go on.” A lone tear trickles down her cheeks before her hovering friend gives her what passes for a reassuring, tentacle-filled hug and snuggles against her tight. Wait, she means indentured servitude, right? Right? “No. I was on the streets with nowhere to go, so I took the first opportunity I could find to stay alive. I answered an ad looking for strong and resilient girls for Paragon Inc.’s new project. Since they promised they would cover all my expenses, I eagerly responded without reading the fine prints and ended up as a new type of XO enforcer.” Her voice trails off a little and her eyes slowly drift to her chromed up limbs. “Since we were new prototypes for a potential upgrade of the XO corp, we were equipped with features such as magnetic, modular limbs that could turn law enforcers into sex-relief units or caretakers on demand,” she whispers. “However, we were too expensive to mass-produce and were thus sold to the wealthiest zaika Peers as their personal bodyguards and sexual-relief units.” You talked about a Peer that owned you? How was she? “A terrible monster of a Paragon high up. She used me to intimidate and repress those she despised. Her favorite thing to do was to make me pin a poor gal on the ground and stuff her to the point she would be forced to cough my seed out. So, how did she end up here? A warm smile erases her despair as she brings the blue floating head in an almost motherly hug. “It’s thanks to my friend Dabebbieth. Could you tell this little guy over here’s a skilled poet?” The sentient head jellyfish’s cheeks take a darker coloration a second before unintelligible words escape his [silly |dickpillows |lips ]. “Isn’t he the cutest leyak around? It’s just a shame he’s so shy because [silly |I’m not fluent in bottom |he barely speaks with his beautiful voice ].” The alien’s blue face turns three shades darker following his friend’s compliment and flustered words cross his large lips. Long tentacles fail to hide his face from your gaze as the melodic mumbles spirit your [pc.ears] to a comfy paradise. Ethereal sounds and tunes soothe your mind with colorful feelings, as if the chant is tying your senses together into a sweet fantasy. Paulette stops the leyak’s melody with a few headpats a minute later, gently ending the mind-flaring experience. “I see you enjoyed this little show. I always feel better and well-rested when he hums next to me. He was my owner’s most prized pet. So she did the only thing she knew to solve her problems and sent me against an entire freighter of pirates and slavers.” Did you manage to take them all down? A small shake of her head reports the outcome. “Hundreds of heavily trained and armed thugs against a lone war machine of a zaika? Come on! Life isn’t a fairy tale. They captured me in the armory thanks to an EMP grenade that incapacitated my limbs and fried my control chip. I did manage to turn at least sixty percent of the crew into broken messes beforehand,” she says in a somewhat proud roar. “They were shot down by a rival gang over Tarkus a few hours later while they were destroying my right robotic arm,” she says with a melancholic gaze toward her reversible hand cannon. “I took advantage of the crash to escape with Dabebbieth and a new set of deadly limbs I retrieved from their stolen supplies.” So they had a kind of happy ending in the end? Is she happy with how she is now? Your question makes her chuckle softly as she takes a heroic posture. “By Dhaal’s torrid inferno, of course I love my new life! These Pyrexxx limbs and that [silly |cow jab |Amazon Treatment medipen ] I found while roaming the Wastes turned me into the creature lil’ hatchling me dreamt to be,” she boasts. “There’s also this cute tuft of hair that sprouted over my egg-chute after I ate some transformative candies I gathered when I snuck into Novahome,” she adds with a slight blush on her cheeks. This calm, peaceful setting provides him all that he needs for his artistic mind to shine. His last poem was an absolute banger on the Extranet. Everyone wants to read more of his beautiful words,” she says before dragging the flustered leyak in for a merciless salvo of kisses. original: You’ve been wondering, what do they do to pass the time around here? A charming smile invites itself on Paulette’s face. “It depends on the amount of food, supplies, power, and water we have left. This life might be better than anything I could have had on Dhaal with my upbringing but it’s not an easy one either.” She extends her orange prosthetics to reach for her leyak companion, resting him on her lap and gently petting his gelatinous body. “First comes the food. My little poet over here has a high protein diet and can’t stop himself from waking me at dawn with a drug-laced blowjob,” she says, punctuating each word with a hint of anger. “Stars, I wish I could sleep a tad later. Most of my food comes from the improvised hydroponic farms I’ve set all around the wreckage. I downloaded some data from the Extranet on how to create and maintain a controlled biome with the remains of the ship’s systems.” That’s quite impressive; it must have been a real pain to settle it. “You wouldn’t believe how hard it was for me to find good crops around this rock. Fortunately, I borrowed some stuff from a sydian dominatrix’s stocks. She wasn’t pleased but her boytoys weren’t very eager to fight a strong gal like me, even with their greater numbers. In dire cases, we still have some leftover biscuits and combat rations I’ve found around the ship or abandoned near the Oil Sea,” she says in a disgusted voice. She might not be a fan of dehydrated or diamond-hard food. “For water, it was quite easy to repair the water filtering system and to alter the ship’s plumbing to gather enough liquid for the farm and for our personal usage. The amount of salvageable systems on the wreckage made it simple, but there’s always a damn nut or screw whose model was discontinued, forcing me to do some [silly |calibrations |improvised plasma welding ].” The chromed up amazon points at a few armored, bubbling tanks near the core. “The hardest part of the job was finding replacement filters for the fuel cell. It needs pure water to create hydrogen and there’s not many high quality filters around, so one night I acquired some from an abandoned deck of the Nova.” That doesn’t sound quite legal. Ironic that someone who’d been turned into a ruthless [silly |robocop |cybernetic cop ] would break the law. The violet lady rolls her eyes, slightly annoyed by the remark. “XOs aren’t cops; they are judges, jury, and executioners. And for my defense, I took this stuff from a dusty supply room, nobody went there for centuries and none of the stuff was connected to the machinery, so my actions didn’t threaten anyone.” Paulette then gently taps her bed and points at the small collection of books near her nest. “The best and most interesting quests are for finding stuff like linens, books, holodiscs, baubles... All the nice things that aren’t necessary to live but incredibly valuable all the same. I think I spotted a [rand |set of flowery cushions |flamethrower guitar |collection of Reaper Armaments plasma blades |collection of kui-tan yaois ] yesterday but I was already packed with liquid oxygen and hydrogen,” she sighs. It seems that her situation forces her to make some tough choices. “Yeah, I try my best to keep the core running first and foremost. The containment shell can resist a nuclear warhead, but it would require a small one to reignite it if it ever runs dry, so I’m always on the lookout for hydrogen to feed it. It’s running at the lowest functioning point, around five percent of its capacity, and it requires quite a lot of fuel to keep it up.” Why doesn’t she look for another kind of engine to maintain her farm and water purifiers? “I tried to, but most of the good engines were already salvaged or required exotic components to run. Sure, I could get an old diesel engine to run the filtering system, but the Oil Sea’s purity is low. It would require more power to refine the oil and maintain the refinery than taping some solar panels and wind turbines together to run a humidity gatherer and pump some liquid from a water table to process the fuel cell feeding the core.” Her life here’s indeed quite busy, doesn’t she need help or trade with others? “Well look at me. I don’t really have the shape of a friend, and besides I can do the heavy lifting and tech savvy stuff. But to be fair, I would gladly have some more company,” she muses, giving a friendly kiss to what passes for Dabebbieth’s forehead. “Don’t get me wrong, I appreciate that you showed us kindness and respect but I — no, we could use a lot more friendly contacts. When this place finally has more settlers... or when the locals start approaching us with peaceful intentions rather than trying to steal our tech... then maybe we will be able to socialize again. Until then, I will be the Ghod’Mluss’ipn for them. I think it means Underworld Beast or Manticore in their language. I’ve left quite an impression on them with this little outfit of mine,” she snickers. If she had to take a more ‘civilized’ job, what would she do? The modular XO takes a long minute to think about her skills and desires, slowly pondering her eventual career change. “The obvious answer would be a Peacekeeper. I’ve got the body for it... but it might awaken some of my past’s worst memories. I could be a caretaker like mom used to be, but my leyak friend will struggle with kids and my altered biology might put a stop to my ambitions. Welder, engineer or farmer could be fine jobs if I had no other choices, but my dream job would be a florist.” That’s... a very peculiar choice; you didn’t take her for a creative soul. “I know right? When I was a kid, I wanted to work in the agrarian districts. Their conditions were dire but they were the only ones outside the Peers and the food industry workers that could see real crops and natural beauty, untouched by the Gyre’s polluted hellscape. I saw some on a holopict when I was a factory worker. It was a picture of a sublime bouquet composed for one of Dhaal’s Mavens — the acting commanders and rulers of our planet. The mix of colors and textures were wonderful and the few droplets of dew added an ethereal look to the floral harmony.” Well, Tarkus could use a few more colors to highlight its hidden beauty, but it’ll be a herculean task. “Do I look like a quitter, [pc.name]? And like I said, it’d be a dream job,” she whispers, a palpable envy laced through her words. “I already have ideas about how to adapt some species to this planet’s peculiar biome and I discovered some interesting endemic fauna to play with. It’ll take time but this little project of mine might come to fruition,” she muses. She sets her electric blue eyes back on yours, almost analyzing you with them. “I think that’s all I have to say about our activities here. Anything else you’d want to do?”

A warm, metallic hand gently rubs your [pc.ears] while you caress muscles capable of deflecting bullets with their sheer sturdiness. “Hey [pc.name], do you want to lick ‘em clean? They aren’t dirty but they could use a glossy shine.” You can barely produce a nod before her grip over your head tightens, dragging your [pc.tongue] and [pc.nose] against her sinewy midsection. There’s nothing else you can do but to lick her toned skin as she directs your tongue all over her beefy abs, collecting the few droplets of sweat present on them and applying a slick, slobbering layer all over her majestic musculature. Despite her dominant grip and gaze, Paulette can’t stop herself from giggling and gasping under your dedicated tongue bath, especially when you hit a sweet spot between her diamond-hard atolls. “I think that’s enough, [pc.name].” Despite your best efforts to stay close to her magnificent muscles, her glistening, sculpted flesh leaves your taste buds as Paulette’s firm grip gently leads you away from her shredded core. You give her a puppy-eyed stare but nothing stops her from standing up and unfolding her wings, dimming the light with her draconic frame. “Don’t move; I’ll be back in a moment,” she says before swiftly taking off and disappearing through the lone hole in the ceiling. You don’t have the time to feel her sultry taste fade away from your mind before she returns with a couple of plates, some cooking tools, a tablecloth, two large chunks of meat, and some sliced vegetables. “Alright then, time for a feast!” Surely, she can’t be serious, she can’t cook on her abs. “I’m serious and don’t call me Shirley,” she grumbles. “That’s almost one of my sisters’ names by the way. A real brat of a hydra. I think I saw her singing in the most prestigious Peers-only cabaret.” She quickly sets herself on the spongy tablecloth before handing the plates, tasty-smelling foods, and tools to you. “Start with the vegetables, they’ll need a couple more minutes to be roasted,” she says from her reclined position. With some deep concerns, you take the pepper-looking crop, musky mushrooms, and endemic potatoes and place them on her shimmering muscles. A strident hiss coming from the food startles you a bit as a smug smile frames Paulette’s lips. How? How’s that even possible? “Well, first of all: we are in silly mode you dingus. Secondly, chances are I turned my synth-vagina’s heating feature to overdrive to generate massive amounts of heat. And finally, why not?” You’ll think about the implications later as the violet pterodactyl indicates to add the meat on her muscles turned makeshift grill. The smell coming from the marinated meat flares your nostrils with spicy scents and fragrances of umami while you turn the vegetables around to roast them on both sides. “Smells good, doesn’t it? Daer worm flesh is usually disgusting and rubbery, but if it’s pickled and marinated for a few days with the correct spices, it turns into a tender treat. It took me weeks to find the perfect recipe with the few local crops and edible creatures surviving on this dump of a planet.” A couple of minutes later, you retrieve the piping hot vegetables and meat from her swole middle, placing them in equal quantities on your plates. “Wait a minute to let it cool down a bit, else you’ll burn yourself,” she says before heading towards a nearby room to clean herself. The amazonian zaika returns a moment later with a pitcher of water, some glasses, napkins, and a pair of forks and knives. She takes a seat next to you before handing you the cutlery with a large smile. “Bon appétit [pc.name]!” As soon as her words leave her mouth, she eagerly digs into the sweet potato ersatz, savoring its smooth texture with a delighted face. Mustering some courage, you take a bite of well-roasted meat, filling your mouth with juicy flavors. “Not bad heh? It’s even better if you mix them all together in one bite,” she says with a playful wink. Following her advice, you pick a small portion of each food with your fork and stuff the makeshift skewer inside your mouth. Stars, it’s perfect. The juicy meat exhausts the potato’s sweetness and its texture mixes so well with the chewy mushrooms and endemic peppers. Paulette can feel the explosion of flavors you are subjected to with a single glance towards your ahegao face. This lewd display of yours makes her giggle as she gulps a makeshift skewer of her own. “Told you!” The rest of the meal passes in a blur of tastes crossing your lips as you devour the grilled delicacies. “I don’t think I need to ask if you liked it; your eagerness to clear your plate will suffice.” She diligently collects the empty plates, cutlery, and tools before heading to the adjacent room, surely dropping them in an improvised sink to clean them later. “I hope you enjoyed this little meal. Salty zaikabs grant an exquisite touch to the seasoning, amirite?” Well sure, it was truly wonderful. You might have to ask her for more later, you tease.

[pc.cl engineer |Even for a seasoned scholar in technological fields like you, these readings look correct. original: The roaring battle tank’s engine starts leaking fuel and acrid smoke after your last hit, a concerning signal as the machine’s pistons, timing belts, and camshafts go haywire. Strident hisses fill Tarkus’s hills while the searchlights and laser sights flicker in the wake of the hellish machine’s last breaths. “EVERYTHING’S IN THE RED! THAT’S NOT GOOD!” The mad engineer shouts through the loudspeakers a second before the metallic plates turn from red to yellow-orange to painfully-bright white. It’s about to explode! Scouring your surroundings, you notice a piece of the Nova half-buried in the sands; the tungsten alloy should be dense enough to withstand the piping hot shards and the explosion’s blast. You barely have the time to [pc.isNaga |slither |run ] behind the broken shielding and to duck under its hardest spot before a deafening wail and burning gusts turn the world into an infernal trap. Your entire body rumbles with the ground and the warm, ablative shell covering you the best it can. The firestorm lasts for long seconds that feel like hours to you; hours of demonic screams and unsustainable heat you have no other choice than to withstand or die. Once the wildfire stops, you notice just how close one of the blasted saws was to cutting you in half — if you stood but a few more steps to your right, the broken tool would have split you in two after piercing one of your cover’s weak spots. Still on your guard, you leave your shelter and contemplate the devastation.

You enter the coordinates of Paulette’s lair and the automated machine surges on hardlight legs towards the south-west, antigrav motors humming with power. Your XO friend’s going to have a pleasant surprise. (Key Item Distributed to Paulette: Ultra Dense Degenerate Matter Turbine)

Green lights scour the terrain under the Nova’s shadow while roars of machinery become louder and louder the closer you get to it — and not just because of the proximity. Step by step you approach the humongous lab, going from a pile of scraps to another in the hope to sneak in and avoid the worst of the automated defense systems. The building seems to shake and rumble from afar, as if a powerful, ill-secured motor rocks its frame from the inside. To make things worse, you notice lots of oil tanks lining it, but you don’t spot any generator that’d use it — why would someone gather so many resources here without exploiting them?
